title: Selliguea likiangensis
intro: Selliguea likiangensis, also known as the likiang polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Selliguea likiangensis include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Selliguea likiangensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to make tonics and syrups.
taxonomy: Selliguea likiangensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Selliguea, which includes several species of ferns. Selliguea likiangensis is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and is often found growing in shaded areas. It is a member of the Polypodiales order, which is a group of ferns that are characterized by their leafy fronds and branching stems. The specific classification of Selliguea likiangensis is Selliguea likiangensis (Hayata) C. Chr.
uses: Selliguea likiangensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine in the Sichuan province of China. Its leaves and stems contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is reportedly used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
